steps.title: Simplify the ratio and identify the constraint
steps.content:
The ratio of length to width is 45 to 12. Simplify by dividing both by 3:
$$\frac{45}{12} = \frac{15}{4}$$
So the length-to-width ratio is 15:4. When the width increases, to maintain this ratio, the changes in length and width must themselves be in the same 15:4 ratio.

steps.title: Apply the ratio to the change in width
steps.content:
The width increases by 8 units. Call the length increase $\Delta L$. For the ratio to remain 15:4, the changes must satisfy:
$$\frac{\Delta L}{\Delta W} = \frac{15}{4}$$
Substitute $\Delta W = 8$:
$$\frac{\Delta L}{8} = \frac{15}{4}$$
$$\Delta L = 8 \cdot \frac{15}{4} = 30$$
Since $\Delta L = 30 \gt 0$, the length must increase by 30 units. B is correct.
